title: Pithecellobium alatum
intro: Pithecellobium alatum, commonly known as the "Bird of Paradise Tree," is a tropical tree species native to Central and South America. The tree has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities, which can aid in the treatment of various ailments such as fever, cough, and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Pithecellobium alatum include flavonoids, tannins, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Pithecellobium alatum is commonly prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture, and is used to treat a range of health issues, including fever, cough, and respiratory infections.
taxonomy: Pithecellobium alatum is classified as a species of tree in the family Fabaceae, commonly known as the pea or bean family. It is a member of the subfamily Mimosoideae, which is a group of mimosa and acacia-like trees. The genus Pithecellobium is a group of tropical trees and shrubs native to the Americas. Pithecellobium alatum is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The species is also known as the "clammy tree" due to the resinous secretions on its branches.
uses: Pithecellobium alatum, commonly known as the Monkey Pod tree, has been traditionally used in various folk remedies in its native regions.
